png

Class ChunkHandler

  • java.lang.Object
    • png.ChunkHandler
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ImageHandler, java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public class ChunkHandler
extends java.lang.Object
implements ImageHandler
    A handler designed to perform a heavy-lifting process to extract information from PngChunk elements from a PNG stream.

    This class provides a structured repository for chunks, facilitating efficient retrieval by ChunkType or ChunkType.Category. It serves as the primary data store during the parsing lifecycle, managing subclass instantiation for extended chunks, such as iTXt, eXIf or tIME, and performing integrity validation via CRC checks.

    This handler can manage any PNG chunk type defined in the PNG specification, though it is most commonly used for metadata extraction (XMP, EXIF, Textual). It supports filtered extraction using the requiredChunks set to conserve memory.

    Since:
    4 February 2026
    Version:
    1.3
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

      Constructors 
      Modifier Constructor Description
      private ChunkHandler(java.nio.file.Path fpath, ByteStreamReader reader, java.util.EnumSet<ChunkType> requiredChunks, boolean strict)
      Constructs a handler to parse filtered chunks from a PNG image file stream.
        ChunkHandler(java.nio.file.Path fpath, java.util.EnumSet<ChunkType> requiredChunks)
      Instantiates a handler to parse the specified set of filtered chunks from a PNG file stream in lenient mode.
        ChunkHandler(java.nio.file.Path fpath, java.util.EnumSet<ChunkType> requiredChunks, boolean strict)
      Instantiates a handler to parse the specified set of filtered chunks from a PNG file stream.

    • Constructor Detail

      • ChunkHandler

        private ChunkHandler(java.nio.file.Path fpath,
                     ByteStreamReader reader,
                     java.util.EnumSet<ChunkType> requiredChunks,
                     boolean strict)
        Constructs a handler to parse filtered chunks from a PNG image file stream.
        Parameters:
        fpath - the path to the PNG file
        reader - the byte reader providing access to the raw PNG stream
        requiredChunks - an optional set of chunk types to be extracted. If null, all chunks are selected
        strict - true to enforce strict parsing validation, otherwise false for lenient operations without interrupting the parsing process

      • ChunkHandler

        public ChunkHandler(java.nio.file.Path fpath,
                    java.util.EnumSet<ChunkType> requiredChunks,
                    boolean strict)
             throws java.io.IOException
        Instantiates a handler to parse the specified set of filtered chunks from a PNG file stream.

        Please note: This constructor opens a file handle internally. The caller must use the handler within a try-with-resources block or call close() to ensure the underlying file lock is released.

        Parameters:
        fpath - the Path to the PNG image file
        requiredChunks - the set of filtered ChunkTypes to load into memory. If null, all encountered chunks are extracted
        strict - true to enforce strict parsing validation, otherwise false for lenient operations without interrupting the parsing process
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the file cannot be opened for reading

      • ChunkHandler

        public ChunkHandler(java.nio.file.Path fpath,
                    java.util.EnumSet<ChunkType> requiredChunks)
             throws java.io.IOException
        Instantiates a handler to parse the specified set of filtered chunks from a PNG file stream in lenient mode.
        Parameters:
        fpath - the Path to the PNG image file
        requiredChunks - the set of filtered ChunkTypes to load into memory. If null, all encountered chunks are extracted
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the file cannot be opened for reading

    • Method Detail

      • close

        public void close()
           throws java.io.IOException
        Releases the file handle and closes the underlying ByteStreamReader resource.

        This is called automatically when using a try-with-resources block. Closing this handler ensures that any system locks on the file are released and memory resources are freed.

        Specified by:
        close in interface java.lang.AutoCloseable
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs while closing the reader

      • parseMetadata

        public boolean parseMetadata()
        Validates the PNG stream signature and initiates sequential chunk parsing. This method implements a "soft landing" exception strategy, ensuring that any parsing disruptions do not lead to crashing the entire file parser unnecessarily. In the event of disruptions, they will be logged.
        Specified by:
        parseMetadata in interface ImageHandler
        Returns:
        true if the PNG stream signature was verified and chunk parsing operations were completed successfully, otherwise false on any errors, which will be logged

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Returns a textual representation of all parsed PNG chunks in this file.
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        formatted string of all parsed chunk entries

      • existsChunkType

        public boolean existsChunkType(ChunkType type)
        Checks if a chunk with the specified type has already been set.
        Parameters:
        type - the type of the chunk
        Returns:
        true if the chunk is present

      • getChunks

        public java.util.Optional<java.util.List<PngChunk>> getChunks(ChunkType.Category cat)
        Retrieves all extracted chunks matching a specific metadata category.
        Parameters:
        cat - the specific target category to filter by
        Returns:
        an Optional containing an unmodifiable list of matching PngChunk objects, or Optional.empty() if no matching chunks were extracted

      • getChunks

        public java.util.Optional<java.util.List<PngChunk>> getChunks(ChunkType type)
        Retrieves a list of specific chunks based on the specified type, for example: tEXt, iTXt, or eXIf etc.
        Parameters:
        type - the type of the chunk
        Returns:
        an Optional containing a list of PngChunk objects if found, or Optional.empty() if the specified chunk type cannot be found

      • getFirstChunk

        public java.util.Optional<PngChunk> getFirstChunk(ChunkType type)
        Retrieves the first occurrence of the chunk matching the specified type. Any subsequent chunks will be skipped.
        Parameters:
        type - the type of the chunk
        Returns:
        an Optional containing the discovered PngChunk object, otherwise Optional.empty() if not found

      • getLastChunk

        public java.util.Optional<PngChunk> getLastChunk(ChunkType type)
        Retrieves the last occurrence of the chunk matching the specified type. Any previous chunks will not be processed.
        Parameters:
        type - the type of the chunk
        Returns:
        an Optional containing the discovered PngChunk object, otherwise Optional.empty() if not found

      • getXmpChunk

        public java.util.Optional<PngChunkITXT> getXmpChunk()
        Searches for a specific iTXt chunk that contains embedded XMP data payloads.
        Returns:
        an Optional containing the discovered XMP iTXt chunk with embedded XMP data, otherwise Optional.empty()

      • parseChunks

        private void parseChunks()
                  throws java.io.IOException
        Processes the PNG data stream sequentially.

        Structural Compliance Requirements:

        • The first chunk must be an IHDR (Image Header) segment
        • The final chunk must be an IEND (Image End) trailer
        • Duplicate chunks are rejected if ChunkType.isMultipleAllowed() evaluates to false
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if any structural violation has occurred
        java.io.IOException - if there is an I/O error detected in the underlying stream

      • addChunk

        private PngChunk addChunk(ChunkType chunkType,
                          long length,
                          int crc32,
                          byte[] data,
                          long offsetStart)
        Attaches a general or type-specific PNG chunk to the collection.
        Parameters:
        chunkType - the identified critical or ancillary type of the PNG chunk
        length - the length of the data portion of the chunk
        crc32 - the CRC value
        data - the raw byte array containing the chunk's payload
        offsetStart - the absolute physical file position where the chunk begins
        Returns:
        a fully populated, type-specific PngChunk subclass instance, or a base container fallback if the type is unrecognised

      • existsChunkCategory

        @Deprecated
public boolean existsChunkCategory(ChunkType.Category cat)
        Deprecated. As of version 1.2, replaced by querying #getChunks(Category) and verifying presence.
        Checks if a chunk is classified in the specified category, for example: Textual, Header or Time etc.
        Parameters:
        cat - the type of ChunkType.Category enumeration
        Returns:
        true if the chunk is present

      • getChunks

        @Deprecated
public java.util.Optional<java.util.List<PngChunk>> getChunks()
        Deprecated. As of version 1.3, internal collections should not be fetched wholesale without explicit type boundaries.
        Retrieves a list of chunks that have been extracted.
        Returns:
        an Optional containing an unmodifiable list of extracted PngChunk objects, or Optional.empty() if no chunks matched the extraction criteria
